name: Červená Lhota Castle
region: South Bohemia — Červená Lhota, Jindřichův Hradec District, 10km from Jindřichův Hradec
address: Červená Lhota 1, 378 25 Červená Lhota, Czech Republic
architectural_style: Renaissance water castle (vodní zámek) on a rocky island in a large pond — the castle's defining feature is its location: a natural rock outcrop rising from an artificial pond in the South Bohemian countryside, with the building constructed directly on and around the rock so that the red-plastered walls appear to emerge from the water surface; the red exterior finish (applied or restored in the 16th–17th century) gives the castle its name ('Červená' = red); the architectural style is late-Gothic/early-Renaissance, with a multi-storey residential tower as the dominant element and a loggia facing the water; the wooden drawbridge connecting the island to the bank is the only land access to the castle, adding to the island-fortress character

opening_hours.seasonal_note: The castle is open April to October only — it is closed from November through March. The GYG private day trip from Prague (t1366075, from $329/group, rating: null — New Activity, no reviews yet) departs from Prague and includes Červená Lhota plus Jindřichův Hradec Castle, lunch, and return transport. This is a premium private group excursion — the $329 price is per group (not per person). For individual visits, travel independently by car or train/bus from Jindřichův Hradec.

visit_duration: 1–1.5 hours (guided interior tour + exterior / drawbridge walk around the island)
best_season: May to September — the castle on its pond is most photogenic with summer light on the red walls and water surface; the reflections on calm mornings are exceptional; autumn colour in the surrounding beech and oak woodland (October) is also notable. The castle closes November to March.
accessibility: Access to the castle is via a wooden drawbridge and then steep stairs within the Renaissance building. The interior guided tour involves multiple staircases. The exterior circuit around the pond edge is on compacted earth paths. Not suitable for wheelchairs or limited mobility.
tagline: A red Renaissance castle on a rock island — South Bohemia's most photographed water castle, unchanged since the 16th century
highlights: Island castle on a rock — Červená Lhota is one of the few Czech castles built directly on a rock island rising from a pond; the only access is via a wooden drawbridge; the red-plastered walls emerge from the water surface, creating the distinctive mirror-image that makes this one of South Bohemia's most photographed sites

The red exterior — the castle's name means 'Red Lhota'; the rust-red limewash finish of the exterior is visible from the surrounding paths and across the pond, providing an immediately identifiable visual character distinct from the grey stone or cream plaster of most Czech castle exteriors

Intact Renaissance interiors — the castle interior (guided tour, included in entry ticket) displays original Renaissance-period furniture, period portraits, decorative arts, and the preserved residential rooms of the noble family; the scale is intimate (the castle is small by Czech standards) and the guided tour covers the principal rooms without overcrowding

Drawbridge and pond walk — the wooden drawbridge is functional and traversable; a walking path circuits the pond edge, giving views of the castle from multiple angles and access to the surrounding parkland; the reflection of the red castle in the still water on calm mornings is the most-reproduced image of the site

South Bohemia castle landscape — Červená Lhota sits in the Jindřichův Hradec District, a region of South Bohemia with an unusually high density of medieval and Renaissance castles and manor houses; [Český Krumlov Castle](/castles/czech-republic/cesky-krumlov-castle) (~80km west) and [Hluboká Castle](/castles/czech-republic/hluboka-castle) (~60km northwest) are the major sites in the same region

GYG private day trip from Prague — for visitors based in Prague who want a curated experience: the GYG private group tour (t1366075, $329 per group, 8 hours) combines Červená Lhota with Jindřichův Hradec Castle and includes lunch and return transport; this is a premium private excursion, not a per-person ticket price
description:
Červená Lhota Castle stands on a small rock island in the middle of a large artificial pond in the Jindřichův Hradec District of South Bohemia, approximately 10 kilometres west of the town of Jindřichův Hradec. A wooden drawbridge connects the island to the bank. There is no other land access. The red-plastered walls of the Renaissance castle rise directly from the rock surface at the water's edge, so that from the pond circuit path the castle appears to float — a visual effect that survives unchanged from the 16th century and that has made Červená Lhota one of the most reproduced Czech castle images in both 19th-century Romanticism and contemporary photography.

The castle's history begins in the early 16th century, when a Gothic fortification on the rock island was rebuilt and expanded in the Renaissance style that defines the current structure. The red exterior finish — either original or restored in the 16th or 17th century — gives the castle its name: 'Červená' means red in Czech, and 'Lhota' is a common South Bohemian toponym (a village name element with medieval origins). The castle passed through multiple noble owners across the centuries; the Neuhaus, Cernín, and Schlik families are among those documented; by the 20th century it had come under state administration and is now operated as a public monument by the National Heritage Institute (NPÚ).

The guided interior tour (included in the entry ticket) covers the principal rooms of the Renaissance residential castle: the period-furnished chambers with family portraits, decorative arts, and the domestic scale characteristic of a provincial noble residence rather than a royal palace. The interiors are intact and well-preserved without being heavily restored; the scale is intimate, the visit is relatively short (40–60 minutes including the guided narration), and the experience rewards visitors who are interested in the vernacular Czech Renaissance tradition rather than those expecting the monumental scale of [Český Krumlov](/castles/czech-republic/cesky-krumlov-castle).

The exterior experience is, for most visitors, equally or more important than the interior: the drawbridge approach, the walk around the pond path, the changing angles and reflections of the red castle in the still water, and the wooded countryside surrounding the pond. The site is most photogenic in calm morning light when the water surface is unruffled; the reflection of the red exterior in the pond on such mornings is the defining image of the castle and the reason it appears on the covers of every South Bohemian tourism brochure.

The GYG product associated with this castle (t1366075, New Activity, no reviews — rating: null per site policy) is a private day trip from Prague with a vehicle, driver, and guide, covering Červená Lhota and Jindřichův Hradec Castle, with lunch included, for $329 per group (not per person). This is a premium private excursion, not the entry price to the castle itself. For visitors travelling independently — by car from Prague (approximately 130km, 2 hours) or by train/bus from Jindřichův Hradec — the standalone entry to Červená Lhota is a fraction of this price and does not require a guide or vehicle package.

Červená Lhota is most naturally positioned in a South Bohemia multi-day itinerary alongside [Český Krumlov Castle](/castles/czech-republic/cesky-krumlov-castle) — the major UNESCO site in the region, 80 kilometres west — and [Hluboká Castle](/castles/czech-republic/hluboka-castle), the Neo-Gothic showpiece 60 kilometres northwest. Jindřichův Hradec Castle, in the town centre 10 kilometres east, is an additional major site that can be combined with Červená Lhota on the same day.
history: Early 16th century: Gothic fortification on the rock island rebuilt in Renaissance style. Successive noble ownership through the Neuhaus, Cernín, Schlik, and other South Bohemian families. Red exterior finish applied or restored in the 16th–17th century period. 19th century: castle becomes a popular Romantic subject for painters and illustrators; the reflection image is widely reproduced. 20th century: after World War II, castle nationalized and transferred to state administration. Current period: operated by the National Heritage Institute (NPÚ) as a public castle monument.
how_to_visit:
**Standard visit (entry ticket ~€8 adult, ~€4 child):** Includes guided interior tour (Czech language; English audio guide or guided tour may be available seasonally — confirm at zamek-cervenalhota.cz) + free access to the drawbridge and pond circuit path. The castle is open April to October only.

**GYG private day trip from Prague (~$329 per group, GYG t1366075):** 8-hour private excursion from Prague covering Červená Lhota + Jindřichův Hradec Castle, with lunch and return transport. **$329 is per group (not per person).** New Activity — no reviews yet. For a premium, guided group experience without independent travel logistics, this is the option; for independent travellers, the standalone entry is significantly cheaper.

**Getting there independently:** By car from Prague (~130km, ~2h via D1/E50); from Jindřichův Hradec (~10km, ~15 min). By public transport: train or bus to Jindřichův Hradec, then taxi or bicycle to the castle (~10km).

faqs.question: Is the $329 GYG price per person or per group?
faqs.answer: $329 is per group (up to the group capacity of the private vehicle), not per person. A group of 4 visiting on this private day trip pays approximately $82 per person; a solo traveller would pay the full $329. The GYG product is designed as a private group excursion from Prague and is priced accordingly. The castle's standard standalone entry ticket is available independently for a fraction of this amount.

faqs.question: Is Červená Lhota open in winter?
faqs.answer: No — the castle closes from November through March and reopens in April. Confirm the exact opening date for the current year at zamek-cervenalhota.cz. The surrounding pond and exterior path remain accessible year-round, but the interior guided tour and drawbridge access require the castle to be open.
